what: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
text: diario provides set functions securely storing API tokens interacting diariodeobras.net system. wraps common functionality (e.g., authentication, GET/POST requests) convenient R functions, making easier integrate Diario within R workflows.

title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
text: can install development version diario GitHub :

title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
text: Store API token: making requests, ’ll need store Diario API token securely. Use diario_store_token(): Retrieve token (debugging confirmation): Make authenticated request: main workhorse perform_request(), sets executes authenticated request Diario API endpoints.
code: library(diario)  # Store your API token securely using keyring diario_store_token("YOUR_API_TOKEN_HERE") diario_retrieve_token() #> "YOUR_API_TOKEN_HERE" # Example: retrieving data from an endpoint response <- diario_perform_request("v1/obras") head(response)

title: R Interface to the Diariodeobras Application
code: # Retrieve information about the company company_info <- diario_get_company() company_info

what: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
text: function performs authenticated request specified endpoint Diario API.

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
code: diario_perform_request(   endpoint,   query = list(),   method = "GET",   body = NULL,   verbosity = 0 )

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
text: endpoint non-empty character string specifying API endpoint. query named list query parameters (optional). method HTTP method use (e.g., "GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"). Default "GET". body list representing JSON body request methods support body (e.g., POST). verbosity Verbosity level request (0 = none, 1 = minimal). Default 0.

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request
text: list (default) containing response API. content JSON, returned R object. , error raised.

title: Perform an API request to Diario — diario_perform_request

what: Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
title: Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
text: function stores provided authentication token using keyring package. token stored (example, keyring accessible), emits warning instead throwing error, returns FALSE.

title: Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
text: token character string containing API token stored.

title: Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
text: TRUE (invisibly) token stored successfully; FALSE otherwise.

title: Store the API token for Diario — diario_store_token
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Attempt to store a token: diario_store_token("your-api-token") } # }

title: diario 0.1.1
text: Migrated user-facing messages, warnings, errors use cli package (cli::cli_abort(), cli::cli_warn(), cli::cli_inform(), cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_warning()), replacing base R stop() message() calls. provides richer, informative output inline markup arguments, values, functions, packages. Fixed bug diario_get_reports() query parameters (limite, ordem) constructed never passed diario_perform_request(). Fixed incorrect example diario_get_projects() documentation referenced query parameter accepted function. Wrapped diario_store_token() diario_retrieve_token() examples \dontrun{} avoid CRAN check failures systems without keyring support. Removed LazyData: true DESCRIPTION (data/ directory exists). Added BugReports field DESCRIPTION. Updated authors list.

title: diario 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-01-15 Initial CRAN release. Token management via keyring (diario_store_token(), diario_retrieve_token()). Authenticated API requests httr2 (diario_perform_request()). Convenience wrappers: diario_get_company(), diario_get_entities(), diario_get_projects(), diario_get_project_details(), diario_get_task_list(), diario_get_task_details(), diario_get_reports(), diario_get_report_details().
